title: Instagram
content: black and white image of students on school steps with principal ES Ingraham. On this day in Washington (August 15, 1870), Seattle’s first public schoolhouse opened. Sorely needed, the two-story, two-room building on Third Avenue downtown welcomed 50 students on day one and 100 within a week. To relieve overcrowding, a second school opened downtown in 1875. The first teacher, Lizzy Ordway, a former Mercer Girl, likely ran the school in line with the common practices of 19th-century education. Video by Washington State History Museum on June 11, 2026. Photo by Washington State History Museum on June 01, 2026. May be an image of text.
